channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Government agrees to rename public institutions after police martyrs' families end protest
item.description: The families of 27 policemen killed in a terrorist attack in Balochistan's Ziarat district ended their 10-day sit-in in Quetta after reaching an agreement with the provincial government. The protest, ...

item.title: Femi Fowora’s The Weight of Air: A Review of Mental Health Struggles Among the Privileged
item.description: Titilade Oyemade reviews Femi Fowora’s memoir, The Weight of Air, which challenges stereotypes about mental health among the wealthy. The book explores Fowora’s candid journey with depression, panic a...

item.title: Study Reveals Sweeteners' Impact on Gut Bacteria When Combined with Medications and Food Additives
item.description: A recent study published in *Molecular Systems Biology* investigated the effects of 39 common sweeteners on gut microbiota, revealing significant interactions when combined with medications, food addi...

item.title: Alex Eala Remains Focused on Achieving Bigger Goals After Recent Career Progress
item.description: Filipina tennis player Alex Eala remains determined to reach greater milestones in her career, emphasising that her journey is only beginning despite her recent achievements. Based on the available in...

item.title: Former Radio Africa Journalist Dies in Motorcycle Crash
item.description: Rose Mbugua, a 24-year-old former journalist at Radio Africa, died in a fatal motorcycle crash along Uhuru Highway in Nairobi on Friday evening. The accident occurred near the University Way roundabou...

item.title: Protesters clash with activists at Jantar Mantar over political demands
item.description: The article reports a confrontation between protesters and activist Abhijeet Dipke at Jantar Mantar in Delhi. Dipke, who is part of the CJP (Committee for Judicial Reform), was involved in a heated ex...

item.title: Ontario Officers Seize Open Alcohol and Cannabis During Hwy. 94 Traffic Stop
item.description: Ontario Provincial Police (OPP) conducted a traffic stop on Highway 94 where officers discovered both open alcohol and cannabis, prompting an investigation into potential violations. The incident high...

item.title: New York Mayor Urges Global Action to Honor Mandela's Legacy
item.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani delivered a keynote address at the inaugural Nelson Mandela Global Leadership Forum, emphasizing that Mandela's legacy should be measured by contemporary actions aga...

item.title: Tanzania Launches 2026 Foreign Liabilities Survey to Update Investment Data
item.description: The Bank of Tanzania (BoT), National Bureau of Statistics (NBS), and Tanzania Investment and Special Economic Zones Authority (TISEZA) have initiated the 2026 Survey of Companies with Foreign Liabilit...

item.title: Strong Institutions, Not Short-Term Fixes, Are Key to Africa's Long-Term Development
item.description: This BusinessDay Yaba School of Thought weekly roundup argues that the biggest obstacle to Nigeria's and Africa's development is not a shortage of ideas, talent or natural resources, but the persisten...

item.title: Trump Endorses Lindsey Graham’s Sister for Senate Seat After Brother’s Death
item.description: Lindsey Graham’s sister, Darline Graham, has been endorsed by President Donald Trump for a full Senate term in South Carolina following her brother’s sudden death. Trump, in a post on Truth Social, en...

item.title: Landslide in Chongqing leaves eight dead, rescue teams battle heavy rains and unstable terrain
item.description: A devastating landslide struck Pengshui County in southwestern China's Chongqing municipality on Friday morning, killing at least eight people and leaving 34 missing. The disaster occurred when over 1...

item.title: Community efforts to combat overfishing and protect Pacific tuna stocks
item.description: The article details the urgent efforts by Pacific Island communities to combat overfishing and protect tuna stocks amid climate change and unsustainable practices. Local fishermen are using fish aggre...

item.title: England Manager Tuchel Reflects on Semi-Final Tactical Choices
item.description: The article discusses England manager Thomas Tuchel's stance on his decisions during the semi-final match. Tuchel, who has been under scrutiny for his tactical choices, expressed no regrets about his ...

item.title: Suna East MP Urges Ruto to Redirect Gifts to Migori
item.description: Suna East MP Junet Mohamed has called on President William Ruto to extend government giveaways to Migori, following the Ol Kalou by-election where UDA deployed gifts like mattresses and gas cylinders....

item.title: CSS Exam Registrations Drop Over 48% in Four Years, Nominations Remain Low
item.description: Official data from the Federal Public Service Commission (FPSC) reveals a significant decline in registrations for the Central Superior Services (CSS) examination, which is one of Pakistan's most pres...

item.title: Canadian Beef Industry Faces Risks from Mercosur Trade Pact
item.description: The article discusses the potential risks of Canada's free trade agreement with the Mercosur bloc, which includes Brazil, Argentina, Paraguay, and Uruguay. Canadian ranchers warn that the deal could l...

item.title: MEMErimar and Inocencia Participate in 'Laro Laro Pick' on It’s Showtime
item.description: The article discusses the collaboration of MEMErimar and Inocencia in the popular game show 'Laro Laro Pick' featured on It’s Showtime. Published on July 18, 2026, the piece highlights their participa...

item.title: Trump Supporter Urges President to Focus on Iran and the Economy Instead of Revisiting the 2020 Election
item.description: A self-described supporter of President Donald Trump said he would prefer the president to stop revisiting claims about the 2020 election and instead concentrate on current policy issues, according to...

item.title: Kay Kay Menon on Acting Style and Role in 'Adarsh Baal Vidyalaya'
item.description: The article discusses actor Kay Kay Menon's insights into his acting philosophy and role in the Prime Video series 'Adarsh Baal Vidyalaya'. Menon emphasizes that acting requires restraint rather than ...
